tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated creatinine, proteinuria, and low urine specific gravity, suggesting impaired renal function. Urine is cloudy, brown, with ketonuria and glucosuria, but blood glucose and HbA1c are low, raising concern for possible renal glycosuria and/or metabolic derangement. Mild metabolic acidosis is indicated by low CO₂. No evidence of urinary infection. Recommend further nephrological evaluation and assessment for possible acute or chronic kidney disease.

display.value: Proteinuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Acute ST segment elevation myocardial infarction
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
